beats.narration: A radiography source can deliver a lethal dose, yet gives your senses no warning. Staying safe starts with knowing the hazard.

payload.title: EXCESSIVE EXPOSURE
payload.sub: Why radiation hurts — and never warns you.

beats.narration: Every radiation we use is ionizing. It strips electrons from atoms, breaking chemical bonds and DNA. For cancer risk there is no safe threshold.

tiles.s: Cancer risk has no safe floor

beats.narration: Five types, ranked by reach. Paper stops alpha. Aluminum stops beta. Hydrogen-rich material slows neutrons. But photons? Lead only thins the beam.

payload.heading: What stops each radiation
payload.note: PAPER → ALUMINUM → HYDROGEN-RICH → LEAD ONLY THINS

beats.narration: Reach is not the whole story. Alpha is harmless outside, deadly inside. Gamma and X-rays are the deep, whole-body hazard. Neutrons even activate steel.
And none of it warns you. A fatal field looks and feels like nothing. Only your meter and dosimetry tell the truth.

payload.sub: Instruments and discipline — never instinct — keep you safe.
